description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a release stage that uses the Docker image defined by the CI_IMAGE variable. Define a hidden .release job that sets the stage to release and the image, and a hidden .release_S3 job that declares an environment URL built from S3_BUCKET and CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME, lists needs on validate and the various platform build jobs, runs the shell command ./.gitlab/ci/scripts/release_s3.sh, and sets the S3_URL variable. Add release RC S3, release beta S3, release development S3, and release stable S3 jobs that extend .release, .release_S3 and their respective .rules:release:* rule sets, each assigning an environment name (RC/S3, beta/S3, development/S3/mr-${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}, stable/S3) and the same URL pattern. The release development S3 job also defines an on_stop job named stop release development S3, which extends the same templates, depends on release development S3, and runs the command aws s3 rm ${S3_URL} --recursive with GIT_STRATEGY set to none to clean the S3 path. All jobs run in the release stage, use the CI_IMAGE Docker image, have no caches or artifacts, and are triggered by the rule sets for rc, beta, development, stable, and a manual rule for the stop job.
